fix(acp): restore custom ACP agent detection broken by #2280 (#2574)

* fix(acp): restore custom ACP agent detection broken by #2280

The refactor that split AcpDetector into AcpDetector + AgentRegistry
dropped detectCustomAgents(), causing user-defined assistants (non-preset
entries with a defaultCliPath) to stop appearing on the home agent
selector. The Settings "Local Agents" page kept working because it reads
ConfigStorage directly, which masked the regression until a user reported
it via Se

feat(cs-prompt): strengthen default base prompt against hallucination

Add 4-section core constraints to DEFAULT_CS_BASE_PROMPT: KB-only facts,
verbatim quoting for enumerations, strict section boundaries, and
pre-reply self-check with a blacklist of hallucinated terms. Only
affects new CS tenants; existing customSystemPrompt preserved.

fix(cs-agent-runner): strip model-injected "Skills used" trailer

Some models emit a trailing "> Skills used: ..." line as training prior
even with promptMode:"none". Strip it in cs-agent-runner before return.

Scope: CS hotfix only. Root cause affects all promptMode:"none" callers
— lifting strip to the general agent runner is a separate refactor.

fix(sys-config): preserve file-based agents config during startup

loadAndActivateSysConfig may run before loadConfig() seeds the runtime
snapshot; spreading a null snapshot silently drops file-based config
(e.g. agents.defaults.memorySearch). Fall back to loadConfig() as base
when snapshot is not seeded. Also refresh CS deployment §4.2 embedding.

- Enhanced layout and page components to accommodate wallet features and styles.

fix: 修复频繁断连问题并添加自动重连机制

1. 延长 WebSocket ping 间隔：45 秒 -> 60 秒
   - 减少网络波动导致的误判
   - 适应更多网络环境

2. 添加断连后自动重连机制
   - 断连后 3 秒自动尝试重连
   - 避免频繁重试
   - 仅在未连接且无信任提示时触发

3. 优化连接稳定性
   - 减少不必要的连接重置
   - 提升弱网环境下的连接保持能力

feat: early typing indicator on message receipt for BlueBubbles (#978)

Send a typing indicator immediately when an inbound message is accepted,
before the heavy pipeline processing (user lookup, session management,
media processing, context building). This gives the user instant feedback
that the agent saw their message. Also adds an error fallback in the
ChannelManager so a dangling typing indicator never leaves the user
without a response.

initial commit: OpenClaw Pixel Pet

- root: T5AI device firmware (LVGL pixel game + TCP client)
- server/: Go HTTP-to-TCP state broadcaster
- openclaw/: agent glue (SOUL.md + set_state.py)

@ 51cdd36595fa766a8e0f5d4f5a92bee514ad4218 for reproducible builds.

fix: normalize /me envelope in dashboard server.py

Aligns dashboard's _fetch_live_op_vp() with the extract_me_agent
precedence (agent > data.agent > data > bare) established in c008435.

{data: {...}} envelope.

新增"猜你所想 A股版"功能 — LLM + 启发式混合决策的股票猜谜游戏，作为智能对话页面的 Tab 集成。

核心设计:
- 6 张 ClickHouse 表 JOIN 构建 5506 只 A 股属性矩阵（行业/市值/财务/指数/概念/地域），Redis 缓存 24h
- 混合决策：前 4 轮或候选>300 走启发式（交易所/市值/沪深300/中证500/老股/江浙沪/ROE/行业），之后 LLM 精细切分
- LLM 输出 `{question, predicate, reasoning}` JSON，Python 执行谓词过滤，yes/no/unknown 三态
- 谓词级去重（field+op+value），支持同字段多档位连续使用
- 候选归零自动回滚，12 轮超时，LLM 调用 60s 硬超时降级兜底

fix(ci): make safe.directory step non-fatal when git is absent

The CUDA container may not have git on PATH if actions/checkout doesn't
leave its bundled binary accessible. The code-level gix::open_opts
with Permissions::all() handles ownership checks regardless, so this
step is best-effort.

fix(router): graceful fallback when WS patch fails on first turn

Pi creates a session entry in its store on the first HTTP POST to
/v1/chat/completions. The WS sessions.patch call that sets
thinkingLevel/model happens BEFORE that POST. On a fresh session
(especially a warm container claimed for a new session), the
session key doesn't exist yet in Pi → patch times out after 10s →
the entire turn fails.

Fix: catch the patch timeout and proceed without it. The first POST
creates the session. On s

Handle defensive ALTER TABLE defects in migrations (#488)

The defensive ADD COLUMN statements in migrations 020, 025, and 027
use Effect.catch to swallow "duplicate column" errors on re-runs, but
@effect/sql-sqlite-bun throws from db.query() outside its try/catch,
so the SQLiteError surfaces as an Effect defect rather than a failure.
Effect.catch only handles failures, so migration 27 crashed on fresh
databases because migration 25 had already added default_model_selection.

Switch to Effect.ca

next load_pool() call.  This matched the pre-#11485 codex behaviour.

## 1. Per-test reset of module-level singletons and ContextVars

Python modules are singletons per process, and pytest-xdist workers are
long-lived. Module-level dicts/sets and ContextVars persist across tests
on the same worker. A test that sets state in `tools.approval._session_approved`
and doesn't explicitly clear it leaks that

fix(nim): bound curl health probes with connect and max timeouts (#1818)

## Summary
The NIM health probe loop and \`nimStatusByName()\` use \`curl -sf\`
without any timeout in \`src/lib/nim.ts\`. On a hung or
silently-dropping NIM endpoint, the curl call can block indefinitely,
stalling \`onboard\`/\`waitForNimHealth\` and CLI \`status\`.

Add \`--connect-timeout 5 --max-time 5\` to both probe calls so each
attempt fails fast on unresponsive endpoints and the outer retry loop
can make progress.

fix(pr-manager): fail-closed on jq parse errors in handler dedup guard

Addresses gemini-code-assist finding on PR #36 (PRRT_kwDOSCjzuM58bF1K).

Previous dedup check (shipped in PR #35) used:

    if jq -e ... <input> >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 2  # dedup
    fi
    # fall through: spawn

jq exits with three different codes:
  0 -> filter matched (--> dedup)
  1 -> filter returned false/null (--> no dupe, safe to spawn)
  >= 2 -> jq itself failed (parse error, internal error)

perf: async buffered audit logging (#18)

* perf: replace synchronous audit logging with async buffered writes

appendToolAudit() was using fs.appendFileSync() which blocks the
event loop on every tool call. On phones running through proot,
each blocking write takes 5-50ms, and every tool invocation triggers
two audit writes (start + end).

This replaces it with an in-memory buffer that flushes asynchronously
via fs.appendFile(), either every 500ms or when 20 entries accumulate.
Also caches the

feat(watcher): C10 events_watcher.py + daemon mode

core/scripts/events_watcher.py (~240 LOC, stdlib only):
- Walks ~/.agents/tasks/<project>/patrol/handoffs/*.json (all projects,
  or one via --project) and re-derives an event per handoff into state.db
  via record_event_if_new().
- Fingerprint = sha1(project|task_id|kind|source|target)[:16] — stable
  across runs so re-ingest skips already-recorded events.
- Kind → event_type map:
    dispatch    -> task.dispatched

test(webhooks/clerk): drop no-op send() catch in oversized-body test

The `.send() catch |err| return err` was functionally identical to `try
send()` — the catch block added no narrowing and no logging, so a
harness-level send failure (connection refused, OOM) would have
propagated as-is and looked like a test skip. Plain `try` surfaces the
same error with the standard zig test diagnostic. In practice httpz
accepts the 2 MiB body and the handler's checkBodySize returns 413
UZ-REQ-002 — confirmed

responsiveness.

Refactor dropDeprecatedColumns function to improve column removal logic

This commit updates the dropDeprecatedColumns function to utilize a structured approach for managing deprecated columns. The deprecated columns are now defined as a slice of structs, allowing for better handling of model instances and preventing potential panics. fix: unauthenticated /healthz /livez /readyz for K8s probes

Deploy debugging showed pods CrashLoopBackOff even though the gateway
started cleanly — K8s was killing them after liveness probe failures.
Root cause: probes hit /api/status, which goes through the userAuth
middleware and returns 401 without a bearer token, so every probe
failed and the pod got SIGTERM'd at the failureThreshold boundary.

Add three unauthenticated health endpoints (standard names) that
always return 200 without touchi

404 at the nginx / ALB layer, never reaches NestJS — verified:

  => 200 application/json + x-request-id

fix(pr-manager): dedup handler spawns, raise timeout, staleness guard

Observed 2026-04-21: 14 overlapping pr-handler spawns for a single PR in
one night, each reading a 5-minute-stale envelope. Handlers would fire
well after the envelope was written, by which point the PR had often
been merged, had new commits, or had its threads already resolved by a
peer handler — and the handler would then burn its full per-job budget
thrashing on no-ops.

fix(acp): fix Linux codex-acp silent crash (ELECTRON-W0) (#2572)

* fix(acp): emit crash signal for all disconnect reasons including connection_close

Previously only process_exit and process_close triggered the crash
signal. On Linux, connection_close often wins the race when stdout
pipe closes before Node's exit event, causing silent failures with
no error feedback to the user.

Fixes ELECTRON-W0

fix(store): normalize RRF hybrid search scores to [0, 1] range (#496)

RRF scores were returned as raw values (~0.01-0.03), making them
unintuitive for users and LLM agents to interpret relevance.

Normalize by dividing by the theoretical maximum (num_retrievers / (k+1))
so scores map to [0, 1] where 1.0 means ranked #1 in all retrievers.

fix(cli): dispatch /steer inline while agent is running (#13354)

Classic-CLI /steer typed during an active agent run was queued through
self._pending_input alongside ordinary user input.  process_loop, which
drains that queue, is blocked inside self.chat() for the entire run,
so the queued command was not pulled until AFTER _agent_running had
flipped back to False — at which point process_command() took the idle
fallback ("No agent running; queued as next turn") and delivered the
steer as an or

fix(ear): widen events() exception catch to cover websockets.ConnectionClosed

The `websockets` library raises ConnectionClosedOK/ConnectionClosedError
on socket close, neither of which inherits from ConnectionError or
OSError. Without this fix, a normal server-initiated graceful close
propagates an uncaught exception out of the events() async iterator
in production.

test(mcp): pin CancelledError short-circuits the retry loop

The retry branch is only reachable via `except Exception`, and
`CancelledError` inherits from `BaseException`, so today it naturally
bypasses the retry path and /stop still works.  Add one focused
regression test so any future refactor that widens the retry catch to
`BaseException`, re-orders the handlers, or adds `CancelledError` to
`_TRANSIENT_EXC_NAMES` fails CI instead of silently swallowing /stop.

fix(cs): use Date comparison in cs-gate tenant sort (PG compat)

The sort used a.createdAt.localeCompare which works in SQLite
(createdAt is ISO string) but throws TypeError in PostgreSQL
(pg driver returns Date objects). Silent catch swallowed the
error, making the gate always return undefined on PG deployments
and the widget bubble never appear.

Use new Date(x).getTime() for numeric comparison — works with
both string and Date inputs.

perf(runtime): cap long-lived runtime caches to stop daemon memory drift (#201)

The daemon is a long-lived singleton, so several in-memory caches that
never shrink leak steadily for the life of the process:

- liveEventHistory entries retain entire raw SDK events (tool outputs,
  file contents, subagent replies can be 2-50KB each) during a turn.
- finalizedMessages / rateLimitedMessages / rateLimitErrors in the
  message-update layer record one entry per finalized message, forever.

fix: unicast mode now works by combining multicast+unicast probes

mDNS devices typically ignore unicast DNS-SD queries (per RFC 6762).
The unicast scan mode now sends both a multicast query to 224.0.0.251
and unicast queries to each target, then filters responses to only
include IPs from the target list. Also collects all UDP packets per
host instead of just the first one.

fix(m11-003): single-statement workspace upsert + proper rollback on fail

Two tier-3 bugs surfaced when running against a real Postgres:

1. tryInsertWorkspace used a SELECT-then-INSERT pattern with PgQuery. The

out of PRs without masking anything we actually

writer: protect math regions in _clean_proof_text markdown→LaTeX pass

the markdown `*italic*` regex across math spans, corrupting the LaTeX
into unbalanced `\textit{...}` and crashing pdflatex with "Missing $
inserted" at `\end{proof}`.

Mask math regions (`\(...\)`, `\[...\]`, `$...$`, `$$...$$`, and the
common math environments) with placeholders before the bold/italic
substitution, then restore. Escaped `\$` and prose do

test(e2e): use sandbox uid for non-root isolation check (#2137)

## Summary
This PR stabilizes the gateway isolation E2E by launching the non-root
fallback check as the image's actual sandbox user instead of assuming
uid 1000. The sandbox uid/gid are system-assigned in the image build and
can vary across runners, so the hardcoded user caused the check to fail
on unrelated PRs even though the entrypoint logic was unchanged.

## Changes
- capture the sandbox gid alongside the sandbox uid in
`test/

`prepare` again — **infinite recursion**

until the job timeout kills them
- Adds `--ignore-scripts` to the `npm link` in `prepare` to break the
cycle

fix(ci): resolve channel_bridge test deadlock that blocked CI for 6h (#2829)

Three test_stream_bridge_* tests used `let (_event_tx, event_rx)`
which keeps the sender alive until end of test scope. The bridge task
blocks on event_rx.recv() waiting for sender to drop; the test blocks
on rx.recv() waiting for the bridge — deadlock.

Fix: use `let (_, event_rx)` so the sender drops immediately.

Also add timeout-minutes: 60 to Windows/macOS jobs so future hangs
fail fast instead of burning the 6-ho

returns in time, so the turn never fails. The browser, however, flipped
the "Daemon Offline" badge immediately on DISCONNECTED, so every pod
restart / brief network blip flashed red even though the user's send
l

fix(onboard): persist enabledChannels across resume path (#1703)

## Problem

When resuming an onboard session, `enabledChannels` was lost because it
was declared as `const` inside the `else` (non-resume) block of the
sandbox setup step. Resumed sessions had no channel selection, causing
`setupPoliciesWithSelection` to fall back to credential-only checks —
users who selected messaging channels (Telegram/Slack/Discord) would not
see the corresponding policy presets suggested after resume.

fix(ci): pass safe.directory to gix via env vars in rust-ci container

The code-index tests use gix::discover which performs git ownership
checks. In the container, actions/checkout writes safe.directory to a
temp gitconfig that doesn't persist for test binaries. Use
GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/KEY/VALUE env vars which both git and gix read,
ensuring the workspace is trusted throughout the job.

fix: add explicit CSP headers to allow unsafe-eval and blob/https images

Next.js 16 / Vercel platform was enforcing a restrictive default CSP that
blocked eval()-based code (Turbopack HMR in dev, bundled deps in prod),
causing the dish photo upload flow to fail. Sets an explicit CSP that
allows unsafe-eval/unsafe-inline for scripts, blob: and https: for images
(needed for upload preview and external recipe images), and same-origin for
all other directives.

reasoning blobs crossed the default 1.5–4GB heap cap. This adds defense
in depth: a bigger heap, leak-proofing the RPC hot path, bounded
diagnostic buffers, automatic heap dumps at high-water marks, and
graceful signal / uncaught handlers.

- hermes_cli/main.py: `_launch_tui` now injects `NODE_OPTIONS=
  --max-old-space-size=8192

Two failure modes surfaced as "Unexpected end of JSON input":

  404 with an empty body and crash on res.json().
- res.json() is unforgiving: an empty body, HTML error page, or proxy
  response exits with a cryptic parse error instead of the actual HTTP
  status.

so snake_case identifiers and paths render literally, matching the
CommonMark intraword rule. `*` / `**` keep intraword semantics.

fix(daemon): dedupe automatic hooks by runtime path (#540)

## Summary

- make automatic hook processing claim session runtime ownership server-side and skip conflicting runtime callbacks with 200 no-op responses
- keep explicit `remember` and `recall` strict so real user-initiated memory actions still surface runtime conflicts
- add short-lived session-end tombstones so duplicate end hooks cannot reprocess a session after the owning runtime releases its live claim
- mark legacy CLI hooks with `

fix: shutdown races, abort propagation, telegram media-group leak

Several reliability issues surfaced in a repo audit:

* telegram: media-group buffer was unbounded and timers weren't cancelled
  on stop(). Added per-group photo cap (10), top-level cap (20), and
  cleanup of all pending timers in stop(). Prevents OOM on Pi from
  repeated photo bursts and stops timer callbacks firing post-shutdown.

* scheduler: setInterval and setImmediate could fire after stop() and
  hit a closed DB. Added i

Replace CLI subprocess agent with Claude Agent SDK persistent sessions

Rewrites whatsapp/agent.py to use ClaudeSDKClient with per-chat persistent
sessions instead of spawning `claude -p` subprocess per message. Sessions
stay warm across messages giving Claude full conversation context without
replaying history — faster responses, lower token usage.

Key changes:
- SessionManager class: per-chat_id sessions with idle expiry (6hr DM, 1hr group)
- Background sweeper cleans stale sessions every 5mi

LOCAL ai-memory on 127.0.0.1:9077. Federation handles replication
across the mesh. Node-4 remains a dedicated memory-only peer so
scenarios can inspect cluster state independently of any agent path.
